subject: Geography
title: The True Size of Countries
description: A quick, practical guide to The True Size of Countries.

hook.text: On a Mercator map, Alaska looks bigger than Mexico. In reality, Mexico is 15% larger.

buildup.text: Mercator inflates areas as they get farther from the equator.

discovery.text: Africa is bigger than the US, China, India, and all of Europe combined.

twist.text: The website 'The True Size Of' lets you drag countries around a map to see their real size.

climax.text: Generations grew up with a warped mental model of the world because of one projection.

punchline.text: The map on your classroom wall lied to you about size.

quiz.question: How does Africa compare in size to the US, China, India, and Europe combined?
options: Africa is larger than all of them combined
Africa is about the same size
